records.ety: [OE. blamen, F. blmer, OF. blasmer, fr. L. blasphemare to blaspheme, LL. also to blame, fr. Gr. blasfhmei^n to speak ill, to slander, to blaspheme, fr. bla`sfhmos evil speaking, perh, for blapsi`fhmos; bla`psis injury (fr. bla`ptein to injure) + fh`mh a saying, fr. fa`nai to say. Cf. Blaspheme, and see Fame.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blamed (blāmd); p. pr. & vb. n. Blaming.]

defs: To censure; to express disapprobation of; to find fault with; to reproach.
quotes: We have none to blame but ourselves.

defs: To bring reproach upon; to blemish.
quotes: You were to blame, I must be plain with you.

records.ety: [OE. blame, fr. F. blme, OF. blasme, fr. blmer, OF. blasmer, to blame. See Blame, v.]

defs: An expression of disapprobation fir something deemed to be wrong; imputation of fault; censure.
quotes: Let me bear the blame forever.

defs: That which is deserving of censure or disapprobation; culpability; fault; crime; sin.
quotes: Holy and without blame before him in love.
